violations.description: RULE-001-A: Controller route must use plural noun path.
RULE-001-C: Action method names must not appear in the URL path.
RULE-001-D: Identifiers in path segments must use {id} as the parameter name.
violations.location: GetById, Update, Delete method attributes using {userId}

violations.description: RULE-004-B: Missing [ApiController] attribute which enables automatic model state validation.

compliant_aspects: Controller delegates business logic to service layer
Uses HTTP verbs correctly for standard actions
recommendations: Remove 'CreateUser' from the [HttpPost] attribute to comply with RESTful naming
Update all route templates from {userId} to {id}
Add [ApiController] attribute to the class to enable automatic model validation
Remove explicit ModelState checks if [ApiController] is added

missed_issues: Possible lack of standard HTTP response codes for error cases given the missing [ApiController] attribute
Missing documentation on whether status codes conform to ADR-005 requirements
reflection.quality_notes: The analysis is highly accurate and provides clear, actionable feedback mapping specific code locations to identified ADRs.
reflection.final_verdict: The UsersController is fundamentally non-compliant with RESTful standards and requires structural updates to routes and controller metadata.

violations.description: RULE-004-A: Request model/DTO classes MUST have Data Annotation validation attributes on properties.
RULE-004-D: String inputs MUST declare [MaxLength] or [StringLength] constraints.

recommendations: Apply [Required] attribute to all properties (Name, Email, Role) to ensure they are present.
Apply [MaxLength] or [StringLength] attributes to Name, Email, and Role to comply with string length constraints.
Apply [EmailAddress] attribute to the Email property for format validation.

missed_issues: Potential missing [RegularExpression] attribute for password complexity requirements if not handled elsewhere
Lack of [DataType] attributes for properties like Email if standard Data Annotations are insufficient
reflection.quality_notes: The analysis is highly accurate and directly maps code deficiencies to specific ADR requirements; however, it assumes a standard C# MVC/WebAPI environment which is consistent with the filename path.
reflection.final_verdict: The file is severely non-compliant with standard input validation architectural patterns and requires immediate remediation to meet project security standards.

compliant_aspects: Used Data Annotations for all properties
Applied [MaxLength] or [EmailAddress] constraints to all string fields as per ADR-004-D

missed_issues: Analysis lacks verification of XML documentation comments required for DTO public APIs
No check for property naming conventions (PascalCase) defined in style guidelines
Absence of validation for nullable reference types configuration
reflection.quality_notes: The analysis is concise and addresses technical constraints well, but it focuses exclusively on data annotations and fails to audit documentation or broader architectural standards.
reflection.final_verdict: The DTO is fully compliant with data constraint requirements, though it lacks verification against documentation and code style standards.

compliant_aspects: The class follows standard domain model structure.
The class maintains a clear separation from HTTP or infrastructure concerns.

missed_issues: Lack of validation regarding adherence to specific ADR-mandated base classes or interfaces (e.g., IAggregateRoot, IEntity).
Missing check for proper data annotations or attribute usage for ORM mapping consistency.
No verification of documentation or XML comments compliance.
reflection.quality_notes: The analysis is technically accurate but superficial, as it lacks verification against specific project-defined ADR constraints or coding standards.
reflection.final_verdict: The file structure is healthy and follows general best practices, though further verification against specific architectural constraints is recommended.

compliant_aspects: Used dependency injection for service registration using an interface abstraction (IUserService).
Configured standard ASP.NET Core pipeline components correctly.
No business logic or violations of architectural layers introduced in the entry point.

missed_issues: Lack of validation regarding logging configuration compliance
Missing assessment of environment-specific configuration overrides (appsettings.json vs environment variables)
No verification of global error handling middleware implementation
reflection.quality_notes: The analysis is technically sound but superficial, focusing on high-level patterns while ignoring security and operational robustness typically required in an entry point file.
reflection.final_verdict: The file maintains strong adherence to basic architectural principles and dependency injection standards.

compliant_aspects: Clean service interface definition without leaking HTTP concerns (ADR-005-C)
Uses appropriate DTOs for request models (ADR-005-C)

missed_issues: Missing validation of XML documentation requirements per ADR-002
Potential lack of asynchronous suffix consistency check
Missing namespace organizational alignment check against ADR-001
reflection.quality_notes: The analysis is concise and correctly identifies core architectural patterns, but it is too narrow in scope and fails to audit documentation or broader project-wide naming conventions.
reflection.final_verdict: The file maintains strong architectural adherence to core service design principles, ensuring a clean separation of concerns.

violations.description: RULE-005-D: Data access logic resides directly in the Service class instead of a Repository.

compliant_aspects: Service adheres to the business logic layer role by receiving DTOs and returning domain models/tasks
No HTTP abstractions (HttpContext/HttpRequest) used in the service layer
Dependencies follow a standard C# namespace and interface pattern
recommendations: Refactor data access operations (Dictionary _store) into a dedicated Repository class (e.g., IUserRepository) and inject it into the UserService constructor.
Remove static data stores from the service class to ensure proper lifecycle management and dependency injection.

missed_issues: Lack of unit test coverage verification
Missing check for proper transaction handling which often accompanies data access logic
Dependency injection lifetime scope (e.g., scoped vs transient) is not addressed in the recommendations
reflection.quality_notes: The analysis is highly accurate and provides clear, actionable remediation steps; it correctly identifies the architectural violation regarding separation of concerns.
reflection.final_verdict: The file requires significant refactoring to align with ADR-005, specifically regarding the extraction of data access logic into a repository layer.
